title: osmundastrum cinnamomeum var. imbricata
intro: Osmundastrum cinnamomeum var. imbricata, also known as the cinnamon fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Osmundaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Osmundastrum cinnamomeum var. imbricata include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the leaves and rhizomes in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
